title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. frigens
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. frigens, also known as the lady's mantle, is a subvariety of the common lady's mantle plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family and native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, particularly those related to the digestive system, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of A. vulgaris subvar. frigens include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and astringent properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing menstrual pain, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support menstrual health, calm digestive discomfort, and promote overall well-being.
